title: Class-specific loot affinity and starting gear
problem: All classes share an identical loot pool — a Necromancer has the same drop distribution as a Barbarian. No class has weighted affinity toward its thematic gear (e.g. wands/staves for Necro, axes/maces for Barb), and there are no class-locked starting items.

lineage.spawnReason: deferred scope: class loot weighting was explicitly cut from the initial class system to keep the loot engine simple

analysis: First-time players of a new class started with a completely empty bag, no weapon, no potions. This made the early floors disproportionately punishing and gave no tactile differentiation between classes. Each class deserved a starting kit that reflects their archetype.
implementation: Added forceBaseId?: string to GenerateOptions in src/engine/loot.ts so generateItem() can pin a specific base item. Added generateStartingItems(classId: string): Item[] with deterministic per-class RNG seeds (warrior=0xca1101, rogue=0xca1102, sorcerer=0xca1103). Warrior gets a magic short_sword + 2 hp potions; Rogue gets a magic dagger + 2 hp potions; Sorcerer gets a magic short_sword + 1 hp potion + 2 mana potions. In startRun() (gameStore.ts), detects isFirstClassRun by checking classesPlayed; if true, calls generateStartingItems and adds items to inventory.

decisions.decision: Per-class deterministic RNG seeds (0xca1101 etc.) instead of a runtime seed
decisions.rationale: Starting items must be identical across reinstalls and devices — no runtime entropy allowed for this path.

decisions.decision: Starting kit is only given on the first run with a class, not every death
decisions.rationale: After the first run the player has stash items available. Re-gifting every run would make starting gear feel disposable and devalue the stash.

decisions.decision: forceBaseId option added to generateItem() rather than building a separate code path
decisions.rationale: Reuses the full magic item generation pipeline (affixes, stats, display name) with only the base item pinned.

successCriteria: First warrior run starts with a magic sword and 2 hp potions. First rogue run starts with a magic dagger and 2 hp potions. First sorcerer run starts with a magic sword, 1 hp potion, 2 mana potions. Second run with the same class starts empty. generateStartingItems is deterministic across calls.
knownLimitations: Starting item quality (magic) is fixed — could be randomized within magic tier in a future iteration
Sorcerer gets a sword rather than a staff because staves are not yet implemented as a distinct base type
userGuide: Each class begins their very first run with a class-themed starting kit: warriors get a magic sword, rogues get a magic dagger, and sorcerers get a magic sword with extra mana potions. On subsequent runs, you rely on your stash and equipped gear.

publicInterface.name: generateStartingItems
publicInterface.description: Returns a deterministic starting item kit for the given class. Uses class-specific RNG seeds so output is identical across installs.

publicInterface.name: generateItem (forceBaseId option)
publicInterface.type: (rng: Rng, floor: number, opts?: GenerateOptions) => Item
publicInterface.description: forceBaseId in opts pins the base item selection, used by generateStartingItems to guarantee class-specific weapons.

implementationNotes: CLASS_SEEDS map in loot.ts: warrior=0xca1101, rogue=0xca1102, sorcerer=0xca1103; fallback 0xca1100 for unknown classes
